channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Bitcoin steadies at $62,600 as South Koreans flee stocks rout for crypto
item.description: Bitcoin consolidated after Monday's selloff as Donald Trump threatened more Iran strikes, gold extended its slide and a KOSPI index meltdown sent South Korean crypto volumes soaring....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: XRP and ether bulls are getting louder as prices fall, signaling more trouble ahead
item.description: Social media chatter turned most bullish on XRP in five weeks even as the token fell, a setup that has historically favored sellers....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: Trump puts Senate on a 24-day clock to find 60 votes for America’s crypto CLARITY Act rulebook
item.description: The White House is increasing pressure on the Senate to pass the CLARITY Act before lawmakers leave Washington for their August recess. On July 13, President Donald Trump urged the US Congress to appr...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.title: Grok Build Uploads Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Reads
item.description:
xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed.
A researcher publ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bitcoin price has less than 8 hours to outrun $60,000 before Hormuz conflict takes over again
item.description: Bitcoin is trading near $62,172, down roughly 3.1% after a day that swung between an intraday high of $64,273 and a low of $61,794. Now, three separate catalysts arrive within the next 24 hours: June ...

item.title: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
item.description: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Live updates: Bitcoin holds $62,600 as the Iran conflict reignites and CPI looms

item.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
item.description:
Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform.
Th...

item.title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
item.description: A PNG hiding a prompt injection could steal your repo's secrets, researchers demonstrate. The technique, dubbed 'Ghostcommit,' slipped past AI code reviewers CodeRabbit and Bugbot, which never open im...
